issues.description:
BDD acceptance scenarios are pending/not executed. Both scenarios show traceback errors:
  - test_a_run_over_an_empty_corpus_publishes_nothing_and_completes_cleanly
  - test_a_pathtraversal_filename_in_the_corpus_cannot_make_the_pipeline_read_outside_the_corpus_root

Pending status with 'All traceback entries are hidden' suggests exceptions during setup or execution. The spec_gap shows executed_count: 0 out of 2 ground truth scenarios.
issues.suggestion: Run BDD scenarios with --full-trace to diagnose the setup/execution errors. Ensure the scenarios can execute and verify the path-traversal safety and empty-corpus handling in an integrated context. Unit tests pass but lack end-to-end acceptance evidence.

issues.description:
Honesty verification flagged three files claimed in completion_promises that were NOT modified this turn per git status:
  - src/fleet_memory/reindex/__init__.py
  - src/fleet_memory/settings.py
  - tests/unit/reindex/test_walker.py

These files may satisfy requirements (the automated checker verified them), but claiming them as implementation_files when they weren't modified this turn creates reporting confusion.
issues.suggestion: Clarify which files were created/modified this turn versus which existed from baseline. Update the report to accurately reflect only the work done in this turn (appears to be only walker.py modifications).

issues.description: Wiring evidence is absent: status='parse_degraded' with findings=[] and walker.py in degraded_files. Cannot independently verify the walker module's integration points.
issues.suggestion: Advisory only - the parse degradation may resolve when dependencies are complete. Monitor in subsequent turns.

criteria_verification.notes: Both files exist per automated requirements checker. walker.py was modified this turn; __init__.py exists but wasn't modified (may be from baseline).
Independent tests confirm: test_walk_corpus_yields_markdown_documents PASSED. Function signature and behavior verified by unit tests.

Unit tests pass (test_path_traversal_entry_not_read, test_symlink_escape_not_read) but BDD scenario 'test_a_pathtraversal_filename_in_the_corpus_cannot_make_the_pipeline_read_outside_the_corpus_root' is pending. ABSENT end-to-end acceptance signal per guard #1.

Unit test passes (test_empty_corpus_yields_nothing) but BDD scenario 'test_a_run_over_an_empty_corpus_publishes_nothing_and_completes_cleanly' is pending. ABSENT end-to-end acceptance signal per guard #1.
Automated requirements checker verified corpus_root exists in Settings at line 26 with FLEET_MEMORY_ prefix. settings.py wasn't modified this turn (honesty flag) but the field exists.
Required tests exist and pass: test_path_traversal_entry_not_read PASSED, test_empty_corpus_yields_nothing PASSED. test_walker.py wasn't modified this turn but tests are present and passing.
All quality gates passed. No lint errors reported. Player notes ruff compatibility fix for Python 3.14 (collections.abc.Iterator).

rationale: Cannot approve due to ABSENT BDD signal. Per absence-of-failure guard #1: 0 scenarios passed, 0 failed, 2 pending = no attempted/completed scenarios. The pending status with traceback messages indicates execution failures, not passing behavior. Unit tests provide good coverage and all pass (10/10), but the end-to-end BDD acceptance tests that verify critical safety requirements (path-traversal protection, empty-corpus handling) did not execute successfully. Additionally, honesty discrepancies suggest reporting inaccuracy about which files were modified this turn. Fix the BDD scenario execution issues and clarify the reporting before resubmission.
